rationale: Synthetic check requires r1@ == r2@ ==> r1 == r2 for CSingleMessage. Unprovable: (a) Vec<T>::view is `uninterp` in vstd/view.rs, so v1@ == v2@ does NOT imply v1 == v2 (breaks EndPoint{id: Vec<u8>} sub-case); (b) CKeyHashMap is #[verifier(external_body)] with `pub uninterp spec fn view(self) -> Map<...>;`, so view equality over CMessage::Delegate{h: CKeyHashMap} cannot imply struct equality. ironkv works around this by defining a custom `view_equal` trait rather than relying on ==. Without an injectivity axiom (would require assume_specification or external_body — both forbidden), case-split proof fails at `assert(d1.id =~= d2.id)` (Vec) and `assert(m1 =~= m2)` (CMessage::Delegate uninterp view). Tried empty body, full case-split with arrow accessors, and =~= variants; all fail at the same uninterp barrier.
